title: Close custom-op migrations by fine-grained operator unit
symptom: A custom-op migration can appear closed when tracked by broad families such as sampling, interpolate, group_points, and ball_query, while distinct exported/native units and autograd backward paths remain unproven.
root_cause: Family-level closure collapses multiple public/native operator boundaries. In the PointNet2 evidence, four families contain nine required units, each with its own unit_identity, native symbols, kernel functions, launch sites, public route mapping, source evidence, runtime coverage, parity, performance, and no-fallback proof.
fix_steps: 1. Search source files, bindings, Python wrappers, autograd forward/backward paths, aliases, launch sites, setup/build files, and tests before declaring custom-op discovery complete.

2. Enumerate public/native boundary units, not families. For PointNet2 this means nine units: gather_points, gather_points_grad, furthest_point_sampling, three_nn, three_interpolate, three_interpolate_grad, group_points, group_points_grad, and ball_query.

3. Assign each unit a stable unit_identity that includes its family and full signature, then reuse that exact identity across operator_inventory.json, migration_manifest.json, runtime_coverage.json, performance.json, and custom_op_final_gate.json.

4. For every inventory row, record family, variant_or_signature, native_operator_symbols, kernel_functions, kernel_launch_sites, source_evidence, and public_entry_mapping with python_public_api, autograd_function, and high_level_route where applicable.
5. Build migration_manifest.json required_units from the fine-grained unit identities, not from broad family names.

6. Preserve the Python public APIs and extension symbol names while replacing or adapting the backend implementation, so calls through pointnet2_ops.pointnet2_utils and pointnet2_ops._ext still hit the intended unit.

7. Execute validation through public/framework routes, including backward/autograd routes, and record same-run runtime_coverage for every unit with executed=true, runtime_device=npu, and cpu_or_cuda_routed=false.
8. Write performance.json with a performance entry for every unit_identity plus an overall public API route proof showing all units were replaced.

9. Require each final-gate row to carry adapter evidence, OPP/custom-op artifact evidence, parity evidence, integration evidence, runtime coverage, performance evidence, and no_fallback_no_zero_call_no_builtin_contamination evidence.

10. Fail closed unless inventory_count, manifest_entries, and closed_pass_entries match, remaining_entries is 0, full_migration_status is FULL_PASS, and all required units are present, executed, and have performance evidence.
affected_patterns: Family-only inventory rows such as sampling, interpolate, group_points, or ball_query.
Manifest required_units that list operator families instead of full unit_identity signatures.
Python autograd wrappers that hide native backward units, such as _ext.gather_points_grad, _ext.three_interpolate_grad, and _ext.group_points_grad.
Native bindings exporting multiple symbols from one extension module, such as pointnet2_ops._ext.
Runtime, parity, or performance reports that cannot be correlated back to the same unit_identity used in inventory and manifest.
Zero-call, direct-only, builtin-only, CPU/CUDA-routed, report-only, or benchmark-only evidence for active custom-op rows.

code_changes.before: Family-level closure by broad groups such as sampling, interpolate, group_points, and ball_query was insufficient.
code_changes.after:
REQUIRED_UNITS = [
  "sampling:gather_points(points[B,C,N], idx[B,npoint])->out[B,C,npoint]",
  "sampling:gather_points_grad(grad_out[B,C,npoint], idx[B,npoint], N)->grad_features[B,C,N]",
  "sampling:furthest_point_sampling(xyz[B,N,3], npoint)->idx[B,npoint]",
  "interpolate:three_nn(unknown[B,n,3], known[B,m,3])->dist[B,n,3],idx[B,n,3]",
  "interpolate:three_interpolate(features[B,C,m], idx[B,n,3], weight[B,n,3])->out[B,C,n]",
  "interpolate:three_interpolate_grad(grad_out[B,C,n], idx[B,n,3], weight[B,n,3], m)->grad_features[B,C,m]",
  "group_points:group_points(features[B,C,N], idx[B,npoint,nsample])->out[B,C,npoint,nsample]",
  "group_points:group_points_grad(grad_out[B,C,npoint,nsample], idx[B,npoint,nsample], N)->grad_features[B,C,N]",
  "ball_query:ball_query(new_xyz[B,npoint,3], xyz[B,N,3], radius, nsample)->idx[B,npoint,nsample]"
]

code_changes.before: A family-only sampling row would merge gather_points, gather_points_grad, and furthest_point_sampling.
A manifest built from family names would not prove closure for the nine PointNet2 operator units.
code_changes.after:
{
  "required_units": [
    "sampling:gather_points(points[B,C,N], idx[B,npoint])->out[B,C,npoint]",
    "sampling:gather_points_grad(grad_out[B,C,npoint], idx[B,npoint], N)->grad_features[B,C,N]",
    "sampling:furthest_point_sampling(xyz[B,N,3], npoint)->idx[B,npoint]",
    "interpolate:three_nn(unknown[B,n,3], known[B,m,3])->dist[B,n,3],idx[B,n,3]",
    "interpolate:three_interpolate(features[B,C,m], idx[B,n,3], weight[B,n,3])->out[B,C,n]",
    "interpolate:three_interpolate_grad(grad_out[B,C,n], idx[B,n,3], weight[B,n,3], m)->grad_features[B,C,m]",
    "group_points:group_points(features[B,C,N], idx[B,npoint,nsample])->out[B,C,npoint,nsample]",
    "group_points:group_points_grad(grad_out[B,C,npoint,nsample], idx[B,npoint,nsample], N)->grad_features[B,C,N]",
    "ball_query:ball_query(new_xyz[B,npoint,3], xyz[B,N,3], radius, nsample)->idx[B,npoint,nsample]"
  ],
  "validation_contract": {
    "fail_closed": true,
    "inventory_granularity": "fine_grained",
    "cpu_fallback_allowed": false,
    "public_api_routes_required": true
  }
}

antipatterns: Do NOT close a custom-op migration at the broad family level when multiple exported/native units exist behind that family.
Do NOT let a forward path imply its backward/autograd unit is migrated; validate backward units independently.
Do NOT accept manifest rows that cannot be matched back to source-discovered inventory rows by exact unit_identity.
Do NOT accept CPU fallback, CUDA routing, zero custom-call coverage, builtin contamination, direct-only calls, benchmark-only evidence, or report-only evidence.
Do NOT mark FULL_PASS unless inventory_count, manifest_entries, closed_pass_entries, and remaining_entries prove complete per-unit closure.

meta.artifact_note: The supplied .sm-artifacts excerpts were unavailable, but project-local PointNet2 output reports and source files provide corroborating evidence for the same fine-grained closure workflow.
meta.refiner_reasoning: The validation script enumerates nine REQUIRED_UNITS and UNIT_SPECS with source, binding, wrapper, autograd, kernel, and route evidence. The generated manifest uses the same nine unit identities. The final gate reports inventory_count=9, manifest_entries=9, closed_pass_entries=9, remaining_entries=0, all units executed, all units have performance evidence, no CPU/CUDA-routed units, and FULL_PASS. This directly supports a reusable skill for closing custom-op migrations by fine-grained public/native operator unit rather than by family.

trigger_fingerprint: CUDA custom-op projects with multiple native symbols behind one family/package name; closure must be tracked per stable unit_identity across source, bindings, autograd, launch, setup, tests, and runtime evidence.

classifier.reasoning: This is a reusable migration workflow, not a code transform or narrative: discover every exported/native custom-op unit, assign stable unit_identity values, build inventory and manifest from those units, and require per-unit route/artifact/runtime evidence so family-level closure cannot hide missing kernels or backward paths.
classifier.trigger_fingerprint: CUDA custom-op projects with multiple native symbols behind one family/package name; closure must be tracked per stable unit_identity across source, bindings, autograd, launch, setup, tests, and runtime evidence.

problem_description: The project contained four broad operator families but nine distinct exported/native operator units, including forward and backward/autograd paths. Closing only at the family level would miss unit-specific kernels, public routes, gradient paths, OPP artifacts, and runtime evidence.
rough_fix_approach: 1. Discover custom-op units from source, bindings, wrappers, autograd paths, aliases, launch sites, setup files, and tests. 2. Assign each exported/native boundary a stable unit_identity. 3. Build operator_inventory.json and migration_manifest.json from those identities, not family names. 4. Require each row to carry source evidence, public/framework route evidence, OPP artifact evidence, runtime coverage, parity, performance, and no-fallback proof. 5. Fail closed unless inventory count, manifest entries, closed entries, and remaining entries prove complete closure.
name: close-custom-op-migrations-by-fine-grained-unit
